codeReview.summary: All four security changes are correctly implemented in main.js. The OutOfBlinkCors bypass is fully removed. A well-structured isAllowedHostname() helper replaces all url.includes() calls in both will-navigate and setWindowOpenHandler handlers. The hardcoded Chrome/120 user-agent is replaced with dynamic construction from session.getUserAgent(). The implementation is clean, matches the security-worker skill's prescribed patterns exactly, and the worker followed procedure with no deviations.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The security-worker SKILL.md was updated to include the exact patterns used in this feature (isAllowedHostname helper, dynamic user-agent pattern). This was done in a prior feature (ipc-input-validation) and the worker correctly followed the skill's prescribed patterns. No new knowledge gaps discovered.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md sections 'Hostname Checking Pattern' and 'Dynamic User-Agent Pattern' match the implementation in main.js lines 10-15 and 318-322.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md already documents the hostname checking convention ('Hostname checks use parsed .hostname property, never includes() on raw URL strings') and the dynamic user-agent convention ('User-agent construction is dynamic, derived from Electron\'s session API'). The worker followed both conventions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md 'main.js Changes' section lines 6-7. Implementation in main.js uses new URL(urlString).hostname (line 12) and youtubeSession.getUserAgent() (line 318).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the security-worker skill procedure exactly: read target files, implemented the fix using prescribed patterns, ran full test suite (426 pass), ran lint (0 errors after fixing formatting), ran grep verification. No deviations recorded. skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows sequential execution matching SKILL.md Steps 1-6. Handoff skillFeedback confirms: { followedProcedure: true, deviations: [], suggestedChanges: [] }

summary: The cors-navigation-hardening feature correctly implements all four required security changes: (1) VAL-CORS-001 — OutOfBlinkCors switch fully removed (zero grep matches); (2) VAL-CORS-002 — will-navigate handler uses isAllowedHostname(url, ['youtube.com', 'google.com']) with proper new URL(url).hostname parsing at line 439; (3) VAL-CORS-003 — setWindowOpenHandler uses isAllowedHostname(url, ['accounts.google.com', 'google.com']) at line 448; (4) VAL-CORS-004 — hardcoded Chrome/120 UA replaced with dynamic construction from youtubeSession.getUserAgent() with Electron identifier stripping via regex at lines 318-322. The isAllowedHostname helper (lines 10-15) is well-implemented with try/catch for invalid URLs and supports exact and subdomain matching. All 426 tests pass, lint passes with 0 errors. The implementation matches the security-worker skill's prescribed patterns exactly. No blocking or non-blocking issues found.
